checkpoint.worktree: standalone scratch clone, never touching production or Codex 1/2 directories -- see report.md Checkout status

independence_verification.method: clone_engine() -- a custom deep-copy that special-cases CoincidencePyramidalCell (see finding: naive copy.deepcopy recurses infinitely through its __getattr__ delegation to self.soma) and shares one memo dict across self.pcol and self.neurons so both references to the same PC object resolve to the identical clone. Then: step one clone, confirm the other's full state fingerprint (all weights + timestep + spike record + every PC's decoder/basal weights) is byte-unchanged.
independence_verification.result: ok=true for all 6 seeds (c_unchanged=true, b_changed=true in every case)

l1i_topology_note.issue: prediction_column_to_i_enabled controls L1I's incoming-weight-vector SHAPE at build time (1-dim selective vs N_OUT-dim global). The Stage-1 checkpoint was built with the flag off, so flipping it post-clone for C alone would feed a 1-element delivery into an N_OUT-dimensional weight vector -- a real shape mismatch.
l1i_topology_note.resolution: C's L1I weight vectors are collapsed from their existing N_OUT-dimensional values to a single scalar equal to their own mean, applied identically and mechanically to all 9 L1I neurons in every seed. Nothing else about L1I (learning_rate, threshold, leak) is touched; L1I keeps learning normally afterward. This is the one place this experiment did anything beyond a pure attribute flip, and it is fully documented in report.md and lib.py's make_bc() docstring.

processes_remaining: none from this experiment -- every python3 process it started (calibration scripts, run_seed.py x6, aggregate.py) completed and exited before this was written; confirmed via ps -u cxiong. Three unrelated processes were visible and left untouched: vlaunch/hermes chat (pre-existing, 24+ min old), VS Code IDE tooling, and 'python3 run_full_coverage.py' running from /home/cxiong/codex-runs/codex2-phase35-full-decoder-coverage (a directory this experiment never touched, started independently at 10:50:52 -- confirmed via /proc/<pid>/cwd to belong to Codex 2, not this experiment; not inspected further or interfered with, per instructions not to touch Codex 1/2 artifacts).

seeds.selection_note: a 40-seed scan (see calibration_log.txt) found only 11/40 seeds produce a clean single natural L2 responder under 'row 1' at 6000 steps -- the remaining 29/40 show a persistent EXACT tie between two co-equal L2E's. The smoke seed and the 5 additional seeds were drawn from the 11 clean seeds so that 'the natural L2 first responder' in Stage 1 is unambiguous, as the task specifies; the high tie rate itself is reported as a natural-exposure finding, not discarded.

stage1_common_finding: all 6 seeds matured with per-pixel decoder update events between 2946 and 2956 -- matching, to within 10 events, an independently-derived theoretical prediction of exactly 2946 qualifying coincidence events to cross the emergent maturity boundary (350) from init (50) under eta=0.15 (see the previous review round's report.md for that derivation). All 3 active-pixel synapses of the natural leader matured in the SAME step every time (the leader delivers to all 9 apical compartments identically every time it wins, and all 3 active pixels share basal availability under a held pattern, so their decoder trajectories are literally identical). Inactive-pixel decoder locality held in all 6 seeds with zero exceptions.

stage1_config.note: all other parameters left at their committed defaults (prediction_feedback_init=50, prediction_feedback_max=1200, prediction_learning_rate=0.15, prediction_threshold=500, prediction_lateral_weight=150); no decoder weight ever manually set

continued_held_test.description: 1000 steps, held pattern unchanged, B vs C
continued_held_test.result: identical across all 6 seeds regardless of which L2E naturally led: B_l1e_expected_rate=0.500 (1500/3000), C_l1e_expected_rate=0.400 (1200/3000) -- a 20% relative reduction in expected-pixel L1E firing under active suppression. B_l1i_events=0 (by construction, delivery disabled). C_l1i_events=1200 (equals C's PC spike count exactly -- every PC spike delivers one L1I event under the selective route). B novel-pixel firing=0/6000, C novel-pixel firing=0/6000 in both conditions (no spurious novel-pixel activity in either condition; caveat: 'novel' pixels receive zero external drive by construction under simple single-pattern presentation, so this specific check is a valid but weak negative control -- see report.md limitations).

interleaved_four_pattern_test.description: 2 rounds x 4 patterns x 100 steps = 800 steps, B vs C (ran for all 6 seeds since the pattern-switch test validated cleanly in every case)
interleaved_four_pattern_test.finding: the suppression effect is dramatically stronger in the richer, repeatedly-switching interleaved regime than in either single-pattern test: PC/L1I event counts drop by 60-100% under C relative to B (col 1 drops to literally zero PC spikes in both rounds for seed 1), consistent with a genuine negative-feedback loop (PC fires -> L1I suppresses paired L1E -> less L1E activity -> less PC basal drive -> fewer future PC coincidences) that compounds over repeated exposure. Notably, L1E's own raw firing RATE for expected pixels drops only modestly (0.487 vs 0.500) even as PC/L1I event counts collapse -- the AND-gate nature of coincidence detection (needing simultaneous mature basal AND apical) appears to amplify a small reduction in basal availability into a much larger reduction in coincidence rate; this is an observed pattern, not a fully decomposed causal claim, and is flagged as worth follow-up rather than asserted as settled.

ownership_related_metrics.interpretation: no evidence that active suppression changes which L2E's compete or how often they tie/collide -- the underlying L2 ownership instability (documented extensively via the tie-rate finding above) persists identically under B and C. Active suppression measurably changes SENSORY (L1E) and PC-layer activity; it does not visibly change L2-layer ownership/collision dynamics in this measurement.
ownership_related_metrics.multi_winner_l2e_steps_continued_held: B=500, C=400 in every seed (numerically identical to each condition's own PC-spike count in this test -- the natural leader's coincidence-qualifying steps coincide exactly with steps where at least one other L2E also crosses threshold, i.e. collision and prediction-relevant activity co-occur, not independent)

pattern_switch_test.decoder_locality_held: confirmed in all 6 seeds (test3_ran=true for both B and C in every seed -- the gating check requires zero inactive-pixel decoder movement during this test)
pattern_switch_test.description: 1000 steps under 'col 1' immediately after set_pattern from 'row 1', B vs C
pattern_switch_test.novel_pixel_firing: 0/0 rate in both B and C, all seeds (the row-1-only pixels 3 and 5 receive zero external drive once col 1 is presented, so there is nothing to over-suppress by construction)
pattern_switch_test.result: smaller but still real, directionally consistent suppression: B_l1e_expected_rate=0.500 in every seed; C_l1e_expected_rate=0.485 (seeds 1,3,10,22) or 0.478 (seeds 14,17) -- a 3-4% relative reduction, much weaker than the continued-held test's 20%, because only the shared pixel (4) has an already-mature decoder immediately after the switch -- the two newly-active, pattern-specific pixels (1 and 7 for col 1) have not had time to mature and so cannot yet contribute to suppression.
pattern_switch_test.stale_classification: 100% current-correct, 0% stale, in both B and C, all seeds -- no boundary-crossing stale coincidence happened to be captured in this specific test window; this does not contradict the previously-verified queue-carryover repair (that mechanism's correctness was independently confirmed in the prior review round on its own dedicated test), it simply means no stale-eligible coincidence occurred during these particular 1000-step windows

verdict_rationale: Once decoders are genuinely mature (unlike the original underpowered 3200-step grid), active PC-to-local-I delivery produces a clear, reproducible, selective suppression effect: expected/explained-pixel activity and PC/L1I event counts drop substantially under C relative to the iso-topological B shadow, consistently across all 6 independently-matured natural seeds (different L2E identities, same effect size in the two single-pattern tests, a much larger effect in the interleaved test), with zero observed novel-pixel over-suppression in every test where novel activity was structurally possible to measure. That is a real, working, helpful local-suppression mechanism. But there is no evidence in this measurement that it changes L2 ownership: the multi-winner/tie rate is identical between B and C, and the well-documented natural tie instability (29/40 scanned seeds show persistent exact ties) is untouched by whether suppression is delivered. The mechanism does what local prediction is supposed to do (suppress explained sensory activity); it does not, on this evidence, touch the separate, still-open L2-ownership-instability problem.
